meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelancer SaaS Pricing: Solving Testing Gaps (Beginner Guide)
content.seo_description: Stop worrying about missing test suites. Learn how to automate SaaS pricing logic and validation using Antigravity and Claude Code to impress your clients.
content.answer_block: You don't need a PhD in software testing to ship production-grade SaaS pricing. By leveraging Claude Code to generate unit tests and Antigravity to simulate edge cases, you can automate your quality assurance. This approach ensures your pricing logic is bulletproof, repeatable, and ready for high-stakes client deployments immediately.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking on your sprint, the client is asking for a demo, and your codebase is a fragile house of cards. You know that without proper testing, your SaaS pricing logic—the very heart of your revenue—is one edge case away from total failure. The anxiety of 'what if it breaks?' is the silent killer of every freelance career.

But here is the secret: you don't need to be a QA engineer to build robust systems. By treating your pricing logic as a deterministic model and using AI-assisted automation, you can replace manual guesswork with high-fidelity validation. It’s time to stop praying your code works and start shipping with absolute, machine-verified certainty.

steps.title: Define the Pricing Schema
steps.description: Establish a clear JSON structure for your pricing tiers to ensure the AI understands the constraints.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust TypeScript interface for a SaaS pricing model including tiers, billing cycles, and feature flags.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a pricing_schema.ts file with interfaces for Enterprise, Pro, and Starter tiers.'

steps.title: Generate Deterministic Logic
steps.description: Create the core pricing engine that calculates costs based on the defined schema.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a pure function that calculates monthly billing based on tier and feature add-ons, ensuring no floating-point errors.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement calculatePrice(tier, addOns) in pricing_engine.ts using integer math for currency.'

steps.title: Automate Unit Test Coverage
steps.description: Use AI to generate edge-case tests that cover every possible pricing permutation.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create Jest test cases for the pricing engine, covering zero-value inputs, invalid tiers, and overflow scenarios.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate pricing_engine.test.ts with 100% branch coverage for the pricing logic.'

steps.title: Stress Test with Simulation
steps.description: Run your logic through simulated traffic to verify stability under load.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate 1,000 concurrent pricing calculations to ensure performance benchmarks meet SaaS standards.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a stress_test.ts script and execute it to profile pricing engine performance.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Deployment
steps.description: Wrap your tested logic into a deployable API endpoint.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor the tested pricing logic into a secure Express.js middleware function.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Wrap the pricing engine into a production-ready API route in server.ts.'

pro_tips: Always use integer-based math (cents) instead of floats to avoid common rounding bugs in financial code.
Implement a 'dry-run' flag in your API so clients can test pricing logic without triggering actual database changes.
Use Claude Code to document your test failures; this creates an audit trail that builds massive trust with non-technical clients.
Keep your pricing logic decoupled from your database schema to allow for rapid pivots when clients change their minds.
Add a 'Price Integrity' check that runs on every build, comparing the output of your engine against a known-good JSON snapshot.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is live, integrate a logging service like Sentry to catch any runtime exceptions in production, and move on to building the Stripe integration layer.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting the fear of bugs hold your freelance business back. With the right tools and this systematic approach, you can deliver enterprise-grade pricing logic that wins you repeat clients and higher retainers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
